kept.text:
Completely disagree with the framing that Hyperliquid is coming for Polymarket/Kalshi’s lunch.

Polymarket and Kalshi spend millions on sports, NFL, and media partnerships to acquire retail users and pull them into a broader financial ecosystem where they can be monetized across multiple venues and layers.

The idea that Hyperliquid captures this flow simply by offering lower fees misses the point. These users are valuable precisely because they are price insensitive.

The better framing is that HyperCore can become the settlement/execution layer for this activity. Through HIP-4, Polymarket and Kalshi can still own distribution while routing flow through lower-cost rails, paying Hyperliquid a small commoditized execution fee, and potentially retaining deployer economics if they control the market deployer.

For example: Polymarket charges 53bps today, while HIP-4 is 11bps gross, with 50% flowing back to the deployer. If Polymarket controls the deployer, net infra cost is 5.5bps, meaning it retains 90% of transaction economics while outsourcing rails and focusing on user acquisition.

Same logic applies to Polymarket perps: the value is in owning the user. Hyperliquid as backend infrastructure does not impair that, it just removes the lift of creating a performant backend, and allows these retail layers to focus on what they do best.

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is likely to stay on for a short time after his term as head of the central bank is over, bettors on prediction markets platform Kalshi estimate.

Bettors place a 30% chance Powell resigns as a member of the Fed Board of Governors by June. However, bettors are more confident that he does that by August or the end of the year, with 66% and 81% odds, respectively.

More details: https://t.co/vS3IxHsRHc

kept.text:
Kalshi’s 20 Web3 Business Partners: A Compliance-First Prediction Market  

@Kalshi has built a network of 20 partners covering blockchain infrastructure, oracles, market-making liquidity, compliance, and distribution.  

· On-chain: Kalshi connects to networks including @solana , @base , @Bitcoin , and @BNBCHAIN , with data provided by oracles such as @PythNetwork , @redstone_defi , and @switchboardxyz.  
· Liquidity: Partners with traditional market makers like Jump Trading and SIG.  
· Distribution: Integrates with platforms including @coinbase , @RobinhoodApp , and @WebullGlobal.  

However, Kalshi’s most substantial infrastructure is off-chain. It places strong emphasis on compliance, risk management, and traditional financial systems — partnering with Plaid and Aeropay for funding and payments, @Solidus_Labs for market surveillance, and Birches Health for responsible trading. It also sources outcome data from platforms like Rotten Tomatoes for specific event markets. 
Overall, Kalshi operates less like a typical crypto application and more like a regulated event exchange. It has constructed robust financial infrastructure first, then selectively incorporated Web3 capabilities. This gives Kalshi a distinctly traditional finance character among prediction markets.

👉 Kalshi Crypto Business Partner
https://t.co/RgoUJDpgZg

profile_bio.description:
A Web3 asset data platform that pioneers in encapsulating both Onchain & OffChain Data.
